[[Specialist/Legends|Specialist]] '''Cha Raas''' was an [[Agent/Legends|agent]] in the [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]]. He was known as one the nastiest [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperials]] in the [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]].

==Biography==
Cha Raas was the subordinate and apprentice to [[Babel Torsch]], an [[Imperial Office of Customs/Legends|Imperial Customs]] agent who operated in the [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]]. The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] had noticed the growing underground [[Holovid/Legends|holovid]] activity on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Adarlon/Legends|Adarlon]] and had sent Raas to investigate the nightclub [[Glow Dome]]. He became interested in acquiring [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Lytos Urtell]]'s prototype personal [[Holoprojector/Legends|holoprojector]] for himself rather than turning it over to the Empire.

==Personality and traits==
He was antisocial, rude, arrogant and hated almost everyone be they [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] or Imperial. He despised his boss and mentor, Torsch. Raas could adopt a charming and friendly persona as part of his undercover investigations but it was always an act. He was a master of disguise and so it was hard to know what he truly looked like. He was average height, medium build who appeared to be in his early thirties so he blended into any crowd.

==Skills and abilities==
A competent undercover investigator, Raas had all the talent and training to reach his destination, infiltrate via disguise or charm, uncover evidence and then sneak his way back out. In case he was detected he was well trained in [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]].

==Equipment==
Raas carried a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]], a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], and a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]]. He would wear his ISB uniform normally but had disguises and plainclothes for his undercover operations.

==Behind the scenes==
Cha Raas was designed by [[Paul Danner]] and [[Bill Smith]] for ''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]'', a supplement for the ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' that was published by [[West End Games]] in [[1997]]. He was illustrated by [[Tom O'Neill]].
